title: Stale Unix socket leaves the CLI unable to restart the daemon after a crash
reasoning: Auto-start is gated only on the socket path being absent. On Unix, a daemon crash or SIGKILL typically leaves the socket file on disk, so subsequent requests skip `ensureDaemon()`, attempt to connect to a dead socket, and fail permanently. The stale socket is only cleaned up inside `serve()`, but that code never runs because restart is skipped.
reproduction: Start the daemon, kill it with SIGKILL so `daemon.sock` remains, then run any command that calls `request()`. It fails on `net.createConnection(socketPath())` instead of respawning the daemon.
recommendation: Make `request()` recover from stale sockets by attempting a ping/connect first and falling back to `ensureDaemon()` on connect errors such as `ECONNREFUSED`/`ENOENT`, or always call `ensureDaemon()` when autostart is enabled and let its ping logic decide whether a restart is needed.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The only tests exercise `parseDuration`, `EventQueue`, and `isLoopbackAddress`; there is no daemon lifecycle or crash-recovery coverage.
suggestedRegressionTest: An integration test that starts the daemon, force-kills it without removing the socket file, then verifies a fresh `request('ping')` respawns the daemon and succeeds.
minimumFixScope: src/ipc/client.ts and, if needed, the daemon startup/cleanup path in src/ipc/server.ts

history.reasoning: The original evidence paths still exist, but the client-side evidence has changed. In src/ipc/client.ts, request() now always runs ensureDaemon() when autostart is true, instead of gating startup on !existsSync(socketPath()). ensureDaemon() first pings with autostart=false, so a stale Unix socket causes ping to fail, daemon startup to run, and src/ipc/server.ts still unlinks the stale socket during serve(). The built dist/cli.mjs reflects the same behavior. A current regression test, test/ipc-client.test.ts, explicitly creates a stale socket path and verifies request('ping') succeeds via autostart recovery. Both that focused test and the full test suite pass.
